Petition Of Trustee For Approval Of Settlement Made At Mandatory Judicial Settlement Conference And Request Of All Parties For Court To Retain Jurisdiction Over Parties Pursuant To Ccp Section 664.6, To Enforce All Executory Provisions Of Settlement Agreement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Joinder Of Parties And Beneficiaries (Code Of Civ. Proc. Section 664.6)
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jerome Marks for Richard Holmes (Successor Trustee).
James Efting for Carolyn Holmes and Charleen Hamel (Beneficiaries).
Edward Koplowitz Guardian Ad Litem for Ethan Holmes (Minor).

The Court stated that there is a notice issue with respect to Carolyn, Charleen and Ethan, that the last page of the final agreement was not signed by all parties, that the agreement says there are 6 pages, but there are only 5, that in approving the settlement it will not find that this settlement is in the best interest of the Minor as it does not have enough information to make such a finding, that if the parties are seeking this, there is a way to do that in Dept. 514 and made reference to the requirements of Mesa. Mr. Efting stated that Carolyn and Charleen received notice, that he gave a copy of the petition to them and that they are in favor of it. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he has spoken with the Minor and that Ethan received notice as well. Counsel spoke of their years of experience and assured the Court that they have fully discussed the settlement with their Clients, that there Clients are in full agreement of the settlement, that all of the parties signed the first page, that they acted at the instruction of the Settlement Commissioner and that they have a copy of the agreement. Counsel also expressed their concerns and presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to the Probate Court accepting the settlement agreement as signed, approving the settlement with finding of "in the best interest of the Minor", and why they would prefer to not have to go to Dept. 514. Upon conclusion of argument, the Petition for Approval of Settlement, etc. is under submission with the understanding that the Court will not make a finding that this settlement is in the best interest of the Minor and that the parties may seek such relief in Dept. 514. Counsel will provide a revised order. The Petition to Order Trustee to Produce Accounting, etc. and the Trustee's First and Final Account and Report of Trust Administration that were sent out to Dept. 206 on November 13, 2018 for trial will be put back on calendar in the Probate Department.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Siu. Not reported.

Petition To Order Trustee To Produce Accounting, Instructing Trustee To Distrib Trust Assets, And Se
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jerome Marks for Richard Holmes (Successor-Trustee).
James Efting for Charlene Hamel and Carolyn Holmes.

Mr. Marks reported that his Client was affected by the Southern California fires, that he had to evacuate his home, that his Client does not know when he will be allowed back into the area and that the issue of where his Client is going to be living is undetermined at this point. Mr. Marks also suggested setting the matter in Spring, stated that discovery is not complete, that expert witnesses may be needed, that the accounting period goes through October, that it should be available in December and that all of the monthly statements were provided to Mr. Efting last week. Mr. Efting agrees with setting the matter for trial in Spring. Counsel confirmed that it will be a three day trial. The Petition to Order Trustee to Produce Accounting, etc. and the Trustee's First and Final Account and Report of Trust Administration are set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on April 8, 2019 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court/a copy of the order and notice of trial was provided to Counsel).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-29-2018 Continued To Aug-28-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Efting for Carolyn Holmes and Charleen Hamel
Jerome Marks for Richard Holmes (Trustee)

Mr. Marks stated that he is not finished with discovery, plan to complete discovery by August, he and co-counsel will both be out of town in September, and request a continuance to late August for early October. Mr. Efting requested continuance to August 28, 2018 with discovery to be completed by that date. The Petition to Order Trustee to Produce Accounting, etc., and Trustee's First and Final Account, etc. are continued to August 28, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for discovery to be completed by that date; parties to have met and conferred prior to that date. A joint status statement is to be filed by August 21, 2018 with issues to be tried, possible issues or problems, length of trial, witnesses, exhibits.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Continued To Mar-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Efting for and with Carolyn Holmes and Charleen Hamel.
Jerome Marks for Richard Holmes (Trustee).

Mr. Efting stated that the issues are the amount of the Trustee's fees, distribution of $300,000.00 to Carolyn and the reserve amount, and that he believes a two to four hour evidentiary hearing can resolve these matters. Mr. Marks stated that he has concerns about the allegations of the Trustee breaching his fiduciary duties and that it still remains open. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 14, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 19, 2018. The Petition for Order Instructing Trustee to Produce Accounting, etc. and the First and Final Account and Report of Trust Administrator are continued to and the Parties are to return on March 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
